questions.text: To set the dimensions of the `logo.png` image to 150 pixels wide and 150 pixels tall in HTML, we use:
questions.explanation: In HTML, the `` tag uses `width` and `height` attributes to define dimensions in pixels. The unit 'px' is implied and should not be included within these specific attributes.

questions.text:
What does the following CSS rule apply to?
```css
article h2 { color: #FF0000; }
```

choices.label: A
choices.text: Any <h2> element that is a descendant of an <article> element.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Only <h2> elements that are immediate children of an <article>.

choices.label: C
choices.text: All <article> and <h2> elements on the page.

choices.label: D
choices.text: An <article> element that has the ID 'h2'.

questions.explanation: The selector `article h2` is a descendant selector. It targets all `<h2>` elements nested anywhere inside an `<article>` element.

questions.text: HTML stands for:
questions.explanation: HTML stands for Hyper Text Markup Language. It is the standard markup language for creating web pages.

questions.text: Which attribute is used to provide text description for an image if it cannot be displayed?
questions.explanation: The `alt` (alternative text) attribute is required for images to improve accessibility and provide information if the image fails to load.

questions.text: Which semantic tag is used to define the footer section of a document or a section?
questions.explanation: The `<footer>` element typically contains authorship information, copyright data, or links to related documents at the bottom of a page or section.

questions.text: Which is the correct CSS syntax to change the text color of all level-one headings to blue?
questions.explanation: The standard CSS rule structure is `selector { property: value; }`. Thus, `h1 {color: blue;}` is correct.

questions.text: Which HTML tag is used to define a numbered (ordered) list?
questions.explanation: The `<ol>` tag stands for 'ordered list' and uses numbers or letters for its list items (`<li>`).

questions.text: To emphasize text with importance (usually rendered as bold), which HTML element is preferred?
questions.explanation: The `<strong>` tag is used to indicate that its contents have strong importance, seriousness, or urgency. Browsers typically render it in bold.

questions.text: How do you insert a single line break in HTML?
questions.explanation:
The `
` tag is an empty element used to produce a line break in text.

questions.text: External JavaScript files should be saved with which extension?
questions.explanation: JavaScript files use the `.js` extension to be correctly identified and executed by browsers.

questions.text:
Identify the line containing a syntax error in the following CSS:
1. `p { font-family: Arial; }` 
2. `.main { text-decoration: none; }` 
3. `h2 { font-weight: 500;` 
4. `text-style: italic; }`
questions.explanation: There is no CSS property called `text-style`. To make text italic, the property `font-style: italic;` should be used. Line 4 is therefore erroneous.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the W3C CSS Validation Service?

choices.label: A
choices.text: To host your CSS files on the web.

choices.label: B
choices.text: To check if your CSS code adheres to standard specifications.

choices.label: C
choices.text: To convert CSS code into JavaScript.

choices.label: D
choices.text: To minimize the size of your images.

questions.explanation: The W3C validator ensures that your CSS code follows the official rules and syntax standards, helping to ensure cross-browser compatibility.

questions.text: Given the CSS rule: `#header { background: black; }`, which HTML tag will it apply to?
questions.explanation: In CSS, the hash symbol (`#`) denotes an ID selector. It targets the unique element with the matching `id` attribute.

questions.text: The correct way to reference an external stylesheet in the `<head>` is:
questions.explanation: The `<link>` tag with `rel="stylesheet"` and the `href` attribute pointing to the file path is the standard method for including external CSS.

questions.text: To display an image `profile.jpg` located in the same folder as the HTML file, we use:
questions.explanation: The `` tag requires the `src` attribute to define the source path of the image file.

questions.text: To move an element 50 pixels to the left from its default position, which CSS is correct?
questions.explanation: Using `position: relative` allows an element to be offset from its normal flow. A negative `left` value (or positive `right` value) moves it to the left.
Class selectors (`.red-text`) have higher specificity than element selectors (`p`). Therefore, the color red takes precedence over blue.

questions.text: In a form, to ensure that only one radio button in a group can be selected, what must be true?

choices.label: B
choices.text: They must have the same `name` attribute value.

choices.label: C
choices.text: They must have the same `value` attribute.

choices.label: D
choices.text: They must be inside different <form> tags.

questions.explanation: The `name` attribute groups radio buttons together. When they share the same name, the browser ensures only one can be checked at a time.

questions.text: Which HTML5 input attribute is used to specify a regular expression that the input's value must match?
questions.explanation: The `pattern` attribute allows developers to define a custom validation rule using a regular expression.

questions.text: How do you create a link that opens in a new tab or window?
questions.explanation: The `target="_blank"` attribute tells the browser to open the linked document in a new window or tab.

questions.text: Which selector targets all `<span>` elements inside any `<li>` elements?
questions.explanation: The descendant selector (a space between tags) targets elements nested within the first tag.

questions.text: In the rule `font-family: Verdana, Geneva, sans-serif;`, what happens if the user does not have Verdana installed?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The browser displays an error message.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The browser skips the font-family rule entirely.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The browser attempts to load Geneva.

questions.explanation: CSS font-family lists act as fallbacks. The browser tries them in order from left to right.

questions.text: The document's character encoding (e.g., UTF-8) should be specified within the:
questions.explanation: Metadata such as character sets (`<meta charset="UTF-8">`) belongs in the `<head>` section.

questions.text: Which HTML5 input type provides a slider control for selecting a number within a range?
questions.explanation: The `type="range"` attribute creates a slider bar UI for numeric input.

questions.text: What is the correct way to associate a label with an input using the `id`?
questions.explanation: The `for` attribute in the `<label>` must match the `id` of the `<input>` to bind them together for accessibility and usability.

questions.text: To remove the underline from all links on a page, use:
questions.explanation: Underlines on links are controlled by the `text-decoration` property. Setting it to `none` removes the default styling.

questions.text: To make text italic in CSS, you use which property?
questions.explanation: The `font-style` property is used to specify normal, italic, or oblique text.

questions.text: The CSS rule `p.warning { color: orange; }` applies to:

choices.label: B
choices.text:
All

elements inside a 'warning' class element.

choices.label: C
choices.text:
Only

elements that have the class 'warning'.

choices.label: D
choices.text:
Only the first

element on the page.

questions.explanation:
The selector `p.warning` combines the element selector and class selector, targeting only `

` tags with that specific class.

questions.text: How do you make a table cell span across 3 columns?
questions.explanation: The `colspan` attribute specifies the number of columns a cell should span.

questions.text: Which character entity is used to display the copyright symbol (©)?
questions.explanation: The `©` entity is the standard HTML code for the copyright symbol.

questions.text: What does CSS stand for?
questions.explanation: CSS stands for Cascading Style Sheets.

questions.text: Which `<meta>` attribute is used to provide keywords for search engines?
questions.explanation: The `name="keywords"` meta tag was historically used to list terms related to the page content for search engines.

questions.text: Which selector has the highest specificity?
questions.explanation: ID selectors (`#`) have higher specificity than class selectors (`.`), which in turn have higher specificity than element selectors.

questions.text: Which pseudo-class is used to style a link that has already been clicked?
questions.explanation: The `:visited` pseudo-class allows developers to change the appearance of links already present in the user's browser history.

questions.text: The `<progress>` element is used to:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Display a loading bar for a task.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Measure a scalar value within a known range.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Show a list of ongoing downloads.

questions.explanation: The `<progress>` tag represents the completion progress of a task, typically shown as a bar.

questions.text: To display the 'less than' symbol (<) in an HTML page without the browser interpreting it as a tag, we write:
questions.explanation: The character entity `<` (less than) is used to escape the character so it renders as text.

questions.text: How do you add a comment in a CSS file?
questions.explanation: CSS uses C-style comments: `/*` to start and `*/` to end.

questions.text: What is the correct syntax for a link to a telephone number?
questions.explanation: The `tel:` protocol in an `href` attribute allows users on mobile devices or computers with telephony software to click and dial a number.

questions.text: Which property controls the stacking order of elements that overlap?
questions.explanation: The `z-index` property specifies the stack level of an element. Higher values are placed in front of lower values.

choices.label: A
choices.text: The width should be in px.

choices.label: B
choices.text: There is a missing semicolon after '10px'.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The class selector should start with #.

questions.explanation: Every property-value pair in a CSS declaration block must be terminated with a semicolon unless it is the last item.

questions.text: To set an element's width to exactly half of its parent container, we use:
questions.explanation: Percentage values in width are relative to the width of the containing element.

questions.text: Which tag is used for the header cells in a table, usually rendered as bold and centered?
questions.explanation: The `<th>` (table header) element is used for cells that act as headers for a column or row.

questions.text: Which of these is a block-level element by default?
questions.explanation:
A `
` is a block-level element, meaning it starts on a new line and takes up the full width available. ``, ``, and `` are inline by default.

questions.text: To position an element relative to the browser window, even when scrolling, we use:
questions.explanation: The `fixed` position value keeps an element in the same place relative to the viewport regardless of scrolling.

questions.text: What is the correct CSS to set the background color to white using a hex code?
questions.explanation: Hex code `#FFFFFF` represents the maximum intensity for Red, Green, and Blue, resulting in white.

questions.text: Which HTML5 tag is most appropriate for a navigation menu?
questions.explanation: The `<nav>` element is intended for major blocks of navigation links.

questions.text: To create a button that sends form data to a server, we use:
questions.explanation: The `submit` input type triggers the submission of the form to the URL specified in the form's `action` attribute.

questions.text: Which tag provides a caption for a `<figure>` element?
questions.explanation: The `<figcaption>` tag is used to provide a visible title or description for the contents of the `<figure>` container.

questions.text: To center text within a `<div>` in CSS, you use:
questions.explanation: The `text-align: center;` property centers the inline content (like text) within its block-level parent.

questions.text: To apply a background image to the entire web page, the CSS should be applied to which selector?
questions.explanation: The `<body>` tag represents the visible content of the page, so applying a background image to it covers the whole document canvas.
